response:
No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.

To find the speed, we first express wind power \(N\) as the product of force \(F\) and boat speed \(v\):
\[ N = F \cdot v = \frac{B S \rho (v_0 - v)^2}{2} \cdot v \]

Since \(B\), \(S\), and \(\rho\) are constants, maximizing \(N\) is equivalent to maximizing the function \(f(v) = (v_0 - v)^2 v\). We take the derivative with respect to \(v\) and set it to zero:
\[ \frac{d}{dv} [(v_0 - v)^2 v] = (v_0 - v)^2 + v \cdot 2(v_0 - v)(-1) = 0 \]
\[ (v_0 - v) [ (v_0 - v) - 2v ] = 0 \]
\[ (v_0 - v)(v_0 - 3v) = 0 \]

The solutions are \(v = v_0\) (where power is zero) or \(v = v_0 / 3\) (where power is maximum).
Using \(v_0 = 6.3 \, \text{m/s}\):
\[ v = \frac{6.3}{3} = 2.1 \, \text{m/s} \]

The speed of the sailboat at maximum power is **2.1 m/s**.
